title: Will Joe Biden and Donald Trump face off in 2024 debates?
shortDescription: Trump, who refused to debate Republican opponents during the nomination fight, has taken a decidedly different tactic with regard to the general election.

title: OnPolitics: Will Donald Trump face prison time?
shortDescription: The former president faces 34 felony counts in a New York case. While prison time may be unlikely, experts say it isn't out of the question.

title: Iowa Senate approves bill allowing teachers to carry guns at school
shortDescription: K-12 teachers and other school employees could obtain permits to carry guns on campus under the bill passed by the Senate.

title: Japanese prime minister calls on Congress as Ukraine, Israel aid stalls
shortDescription: Japanese Prime Minister Fumio Kishida spoke to Congress as the House considers a massive aid package to help Ukraine, Taiwan and Israel.

title: Johnson and Trump to promote bill to prevent non-citizens from voting
shortDescription: House Speaker Mike Johnson will introduce an “election integrity” bill aimed at preventing non-citizens from voting with Donald Trump.

title: Voter ID and absentee ballots: is voting getting harder in the South?
shortDescription: Since the 2020 election, multiple states have tightened voting regulations that are expected to impact the outcome of key elections in swing states.

title: 'Everyone has to vote': Women on Arizona Supreme Court abortion ruling
shortDescription: Reproductive rights are on the minds of women, from teens to college students to retirees, as they come to grips with an Arizona high court decision.

title: Donald Trump's first criminal trial to begin April 15 in New York
shortDescription: Donald Trump is facing 34 criminal charges ranging from misdemeanors to felonies for allegedly falsifying business records back in 2016.

title: Biden administration's rules for unlicensed gun dealers | The Excerpt
shortDescription: Plus: Bipartisan frustration over bungled FAFSA rollout on display at congressional hearing. Inflation picked up in March. And more top news stories.

title: House GOP halts Democrats' effort to repeal Arizona abortion ban
shortDescription: The GOP-controlled Arizona House quickly gaveled out of session after lawmakers attempted to repeal Arizona's 1864 near-total abortion ban.

title: Yes, you can vote without having a permanent address
shortDescription: You can vote if you are experiencing homelessness, but the rules of registration are different in each state.

title: House GOP in chaos after conservatives, spurred by Trump, tank vote
shortDescription: Right-wing fury – spurred by former President Donald Trump – has dealt Mike Johnson another blow.

title: Will Trump go to prison if found guilty in New York hush money case?
shortDescription: Former President Donald Trump faces 34 felony counts in his upcoming New York hush money trial, and experts say he could be imprisoned if convicted.

title: Tennessee Senate passes bill allowing teachers to carry guns
shortDescription: According to the bill, Tennessee teachers would need to undergo special training to carry guns in schools. The House has not yet advanced the bill.

title: 'The Lord's chicken.' Trump visits Atlanta Chick-fil-A ahead of fundraiser
shortDescription: "That's the Lord's chicken," former President Donald Trump quipped as he made a stop at a Chick-fil-A ahead of his Georgia fundraiser.

title: Why didn't these two Nebraska lawmakers vote for transgender ban?
shortDescription: Two conservative state senators didn't vote for the Sports and Spaces Act because it would overlap with current policies

title: Trump, Biden tied in NC, but Dems hold lead in governor's race: poll
shortDescription: Democratic candidate expands his lead over GOP rival in Tar Heel State

title: Reynolds signs law letting Iowa police arrest undocumented immigrants
shortDescription: The law, which goes into effect July 1, makes it a misdemeanor for a person to enter Iowa if they were previously deported from the U.S.

title: 2017 'Morning Joe' clip shows comment on Trump, not Musk | Fact check
shortDescription: "Morning Joe" co-host Mika Brzezinski said in 2017 that Trump was "trying to control" people's thoughts. She wasn't talking about Elon Musk.

title: Congress puts FAFSA frustration in spotlight: ‘Nothing but a disaster’
shortDescription: Republicans and Democrats agree on at least one thing lately: the Biden administration’s handling of college financial aid this year has been a mess.

title: Trump says he wouldn't sign national abortion ban if he wins White House
shortDescription: Asked Wednesday during a trip to Georgia if he'd sign a federal ban on abortions, Trump said "no." He also said an Arizona abortion law goes too far.

title: Post omits context from GOP lawmaker's Nazi comments | Fact check
shortDescription: Maine state Rep. Laurel Libby was referring to the constitutional rights of neo-Nazi demonstrators in the U.S., not Nazi Germany.

title: OnPolitics: Joe Biden blames Donald Trump for AZ abortion ruling
shortDescription: The president's reelection campaign was quick to condemn the near total ban and point fingers at the former president.

title: Ex-Trump CFO gets five months in jail for perjury
shortDescription: Allen Weisselberg, the former chief financial officer at the Trump Organization, pleaded guilty to lying under oath during Trump's civil fraud trial.
